assignment_description:
Introduction
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
How would you define Imagination? What role do you think it plays in your spiritual formation or growth? What do you think the title of the book means or is referring to? You may find it helpful to annotate while you read. Sometimes, for a first reading, it can be helpful to wait to read the footnotes until you finish reading the assigned pages. Then go back and read the footnote for sentences in the reading that interested you at a later time.

➜ READ
Introduction

➜ NARRATE
Capture the main points of the Introduction in writing. What is the plan of the book? Make a list of 5-7 things you behold regularly. Write one word for what each one might be forming in you.

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from page x, "Looking through the eyes of Christians from the past is like trying on spectacles that remedy our myopic fixation on the present and give us a more vivid view of the fullness of the gospel." How does this work? Why are we fixated on the present?
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Add 15 minutes to these lessons to have a class or teacher/student discussion or for the student to write independently. Discussion questions are included in the lessons.

assignment_description:
Who is Jesus Part 1
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Depending on your particular faith tradition, icons may be completely unknown to you. For others, they are a regular part of your life. Enjoy learning more about them and how they can "train us to participate in the astonishing reality of the incarnation."

➜ READ
Ch.1 p.1-17 "Stories are" - "icons say."

➜ NARRATE
What images have shaped your view of Jesus?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have more time)
• Discuss the quote from p.9, "In our efforts to see Jesus, it is all too easy for us to make him in our own image." OR the quote from p.16, "Icons, on the other hand, are not mirrors but windows."

• EXTRA HELPING
If you are interested:
∞ Video Link: History of Iconography

Who is Jesus Part 2
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Refer to Figure 1 in the center of your book to see the icon referenced in your reading today.

➜ READ
Ch.1 p.17-25 "The eyes staring" - "face-to-face."

➜ NARRATE
Express your reflections from that experience in some way. What did you notice? What did you wonder about? What did it remind you of? What questions do you have? What new thoughts of God do you have? How did you feel?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from p.20, "An icon is _supposed _to feel alien." What does the author mean by this?

What is Creation Part 1
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
If nature study is a regular part of your Charlotte Mason relational education, many of this chapter's ideas will be very familiar to you. If you are new to nature study, today's reading will give you some of the deeper reasons why nature study is so important. Bestiaries will probably be new for everyone!

➜ READ
Ch.2 p.27-42 "A lion marches" - "to a wider audience."

➜ NARRATE
Silently to yourself, go over Davis's main points or make a bullet point list. Write briefly about one that stood out to you.

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from page 30, "Focusing on human set-apartness at the expense of our interconnectedness with the natural world impoverishes our view not only of the natural world but also of ourselves and of God."

What is Creation Part 2
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Refer to Figures 2-10 in the center of your book to see the images referenced in your reading today.

➜ READ
Ch.2 p.42-56 "With their unicorns" - "of earth hallowed ground."

➜ NARRATE
Choose an animal or something in nature, and write a 3-5 sentence bestiary entry. What does it teach us about God?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group or with your teacher or someone else)
• Share and discuss your narration with someone. If they are not familiar with bestiaries, explain what they are first.

How Should We Read Scripture Part 1
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
As this book is a bit longer than what can readily fit in 11 weeks, please read Chapter 3 at another time if you can. It is a fascinating look at how church architecture forms us. There are delightful images from Chartres Cathedral to consider. For today, we will begin thinking about how we read Scripture. Write down a few words that would describe your current practices.

➜ READ
p.57-58 and Ch.4 p.81-96 "How heavy" - "into the Word."

➜ NARRATE
Describe the parts of lectio divina and what its purpose is. Describe Ignatian imaginative prayer. How do we engage our five senses in the process?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from page 94, "It is all too easy to make Jesus who we want him to be, but praying with Scripture roots us in the Jesus of the Gospels." How?

How Should We Read Scripture Part 2
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Today you will have an opportunity to practice what you read about last week. After you follow the lesson plan for today, set aside time each day until your lesson time next week to practice imaginative prayer, daily walking through the stations of the cross using the exercises on p.103-108.

➜ READ
Ch.2 p.94-103 "Ignatius has several" - "condemned to death?"

➜ NARRATE
Journal your experience with the first station.

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
Discuss your experience working through the exercises over the week.

How Do We Avoid Evil Part 1
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Can you name the 7 vices and virtues?

➜ READ
p.109-110 and Ch.5 p.111-132 "You carry a boulder" - "stronger together."

➜ NARRATE
What do pride and envy look like in your life? How do we fight them with humility and chastity?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group or with your teacher)
• Discuss your thoughts on the first two pairs.

How Do We Avoid Evil Part 2
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
You may want to copy the charts of the pairs and add your own notes or sketches to help you define them as you read.

➜ READ
Ch.5 p.132-150 "Pride looks down" - "path to freedom."

➜ NARRATE
How are these vices at work in your soul, and how can you practically apply the virtue remedies?

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from p.139, "...zeal teaches us that love is a kind of attention."

How Do We Become Good Part 1
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
The last chapter ended, "Thankfully, evil never has the last word." Today, we will meet the Lady Virtues.

➜ READ
Ch.6 p.151-161 "Four women stand" - "in the right direction."

➜ NARRATE
What is the end purpose of the virtues? How do they help? Describe Lady Prudence.

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from p.152, "Since navigating moral complexities is challenging work, we often prefer the blanket simplicity of legalism or moral relativism."

How Do We Become Good Part 2
⍞ Materials: Becoming by Beholding

➜ INTRO
Today we meet three more helpful ladies!

➜ READ
Ch.6 p.161-173 "Whether she is" - "thrilling, adventure."

➜ NARRATE
Describe Ladies Justice, Fortitude, and Temperance.

➜ DISCUSS (in a group, with your teacher, or in writing on your own if you have time)
• Discuss the quote from p.172, "Living virtuously is a combination of living into a set, shared form of morality while also being able to adapt to and account for the dynamic fluidity of changing people and circumstances."

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Becoming by Beholding
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Spiritual Formation: Grade 12 — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· After reading the Conclusion of the book, write what has changed in how you see. What do you want to keep beholding? What exercise formed you most this term?

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ INTRO
As you work your way through this fascinating study on the implications of us being God's image, there are some extra resources at the back of the book. Starting on p.189, there are QR codes arranged by chapter that take you to short videos by the Bible Project that discuss elements of the chapter that you may find helpful. Also, starting on p.195, there are discussion questions that may be helpful to spur your thinking and reflection as you narrate after reading. Make use of both resources if they are helpful to you.

➜ READ
Foreword, Introduction

➜ NARRATE
Find a way that works well for you to interact with the ideas in each week's reading. You might want to read and narrate silently to yourself and then discuss the chapter with someone. Or, you might want to write out what the chapter was about as you think through the ideas. Maybe you want to draw a picture or a diagram that helps you picture what was being discussed. Different chapters might lend themselves to different ways of narrating. Find what works well for you, or try something new to see how that might be helpful.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters, journal or notebook

➜ READ
Ch.1

➜ NARRATE
Identify a few ideas that were new to you from this chapter, and either discuss those ideas with someone or write them down in your notebook.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters, journal or notebook

➜ READ
Ch.2

➜ REFLECT & SHARE
Reflect on the _imago Dei, _and share something that captivated you from this chapter.

If time permits, watch the short video and discuss what stood out to you with a family member or friend.

∞ Video Link: The Most Unexpected Way to Rule the World.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.3

➜ NARRATE
Explain the origin and consequences of human rebellion in light of what you learned from this chapter.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.4 and the Intermission

➜ LIST & EXPLAIN
List and explain a few ideas or concepts that stood out to you from this chapter.

★ NOTE
Don't forget about the other resources at the back of the book that you might find helpful.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.5

➜ NARRATE
Reflect on what it means to be "embodied" according to this chapter.

Chapter 5 discusses the gift of sexual intimacy within the marriage covenant. There is also a section that addresses pornography. Teachers/parents are encouraged to talk to their students about God's design for sexual intimacy and the consequences of engaging in sexual intimacy outside of the marriage covenant.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.6

➜ REFLECT & NARRATE
In light of this chapter, reflect on how you spend your time. Are you taking certain things too seriously? Are you not taking things seriously enough? In what ways are you struggling to enjoy life? Are there life-changes you want to make as a result of what you read in this chapter?

If time permits, watch this short video that provides an animated overview of Ecclesiastes.
∞ Video Link: Book of Ecclesiastes Summary: A complete Animated Overview.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.7

➜ NARRATE
In light of the reading, explain what it means that Jesus is the second Adam.

If time permits, watch this short video and discuss what stood out to you with a family member or friend.
∞ Video Link: True and Better

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.8

➜ REFLECT
Reflect on the resurrection of Jesus Christ and explain why Jesus' resurrection provides hope for individuals.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.9

➜ DISCUSS
Share with a family member or friend something that stood out to you from the reading.

★ NOTE
Don't forget about the other resources at the back of the book that you might find helpful.

⍞ Materials: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters

➜ READ
Ch.10 and Conclusion

➜ NARRATE
In what ways did this chapter challenge your understanding of Jesus' return to earth?

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Spiritual Formation: Grade 12 — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters: What does it mean to be God's Image, and why does creation still matter? What effect does this have on your identity and purpose?

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ INTRO
The title of the book says it all. But why the Trinity matters will be the key to understanding the delight. Enjoy having your delight in God grow!

➜ READ
Introduction

➜ WRITE
Identify three ideas that were new to you from this chapter regarding the Trinity.

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.19-p.32 "that the Spirit is."

➜ NARRATE
Discuss the ideas that were new to you from the reading, and share with a family member or friend how those ideas challenged your understanding of God the Father.

∞ Video Link: The Simplest Way to Understand the Trinity (Gospel Coalition)

∞ Video Link: The Simplest Way to Understand the Trinity (Gospel Coalition)

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.32 "A Heavenly Hodgepodge" - p.47 "receiving from the creature."

➜ REFLECT
Explain why illustrations depicting the Trinity struggle to be doctrinally accurate.

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.47 "How Did the Triune God Create?" - 62

➜ NARRATE
What did you learn about the creation account from the reading?

Read this short article from Pastor John MacArthur to learn how the Triune Godhead worked together in creation.

∞ Article Link: The Triune God of Creation

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.63 - p.75 "Comforter alone, but mine."

➜ NARRATE
What stood out to you from the reading?

If time permits, read this short article that depicts how the Trinity remained "undivided" during the crucifixion of Jesus Christ.

∞ Article Link: Was the Trinity Torn Apart at the Cross?

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.75 "As Loved as the Son" - 84

➜ NARRATE
What does it mean that Christ is the subject of all the Scripture?

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.85 - p.94 "looking unto Jesus."

➜ NARRATE
Share three ideas regarding God the Spirit that were new to you from the reading.

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.94 "Life in the Trinity" - p.107

➜ REFLECT & PRAY
Reflect on what you learned from this chapter regarding prayer and the Trinity, and spend a few minutes praying to Father, Son, and Spirit.

Listen to this short podcast by Sinclair Ferguson to learn how the Holy Spirit indwells followers of Jesus Christ.

∞ Audio Link: The Indwelling of the Holy Spirit

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.109-p.117 "because God is love."

➜ IDENTIFY & SHARE
Identify several characteristics of God from this chapter and share how those characteristics impact your relationship with the triune Godhead.

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
p.117 "When Love Meets Evil" -p.128

➜ NARRATE
What ideas captured your attention from the reading?

➜ VIEW
∞ Art Print Link: Christ the True Light by Hans Holbein the Younger

⍞ Materials: Delighting in the Trinity

➜ READ
Conclusion

➜ NARRATE
How has your thinking about the Trinity changed?

Pastor John Piper interacts with several writings from Jonathan Edwards to better understand the Trinity.

∞ Article Link: Can We Explain the Trinity?: My Favorite Image for the Greatest Mystery

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Delighting in the Trinity
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Spiritual Formation: Grade 12 — TERM 3 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Delighting in the Trinity: Why is delighting in the Trinity so important? Why does it matter? What changes has it brought about in you?
